name: Beef Lasagne Ala Pasta & Co.
recipeIngredient: Frozen spinach: 5 ounces (squeeze dry of all liquid)
Uncooked fresh lasagne noodles: enough for three layers in a 9-inch by 13-inch pan
recipeInstructions:
1. Save the remaining sauce from the can.
2. Prepare the meat sauce: Cook ground beef in a sauté pan over medium heat until no longer pink. Remove from heat and drain fat. Reserve the meat.
3. In the same pan, cook olive oil, add onions, oregano, garlic, basil, salt, black pepper, and red pepper flakes. Cook until onions are translucent.
4. Add white wine to the pan, bring to a boil, and cook until alcohol has evaporated (about 10 minutes). Stir in crushed tomatoes and spinach. Continue simmering for a few minutes. Remove from heat and set aside.
5. Mix together mozzarella and feta cheeses and set aside.
6. Prepare the béchamel sauce: Heat milk, cream, white pepper, thyme, nutmeg, and basil in a small saucepan until near boiling point. Turn off heat.
7. Melt butter in a medium sauté pan. When foam recedes, add flour and mix well. Return to medium-low heat and simmer for 2-3 minutes to cook the flour without browning it.
8. Gradually stir in the hot milk and cream mixture into the saucepan, raising the heat to medium-high. Continue stirring until smooth and thick (about 5 minutes).
9. Add Parmesan and Romano cheeses, whisking until smooth.
10. Prepare the topping: Mix together bread crumbs, Parmesan, and parsley.
11. Assemble and bake:
- Preheat oven to 400°F.
- Spread 2 tablespoons of tomato juices from the can on the bottom of a shallow baking dish (9 by 13 inches).
- Lay one layer of uncooked lasagne noodles.
- Spoon meat sauce over the noodles, spread béchamel across the sauce in two diagonals. Top with cheese mixture.
- Repeat layers using the same amounts of sauce, béchamel, and cheese.
- Top with the final layer of noodles and lightly press the bechamel to distribute it smoothly through the layers.
- Frost the top layer of noodles with remaining béchamel.
- Sprinkle the reserved bread crumb mixture evenly on top.
12. Bake lasagne at 400°F for about 60 minutes, or until sauce bubbles around the edge and the top is nicely browned. If the top browns too quickly, cover with aluminum foil during the last 10 minutes of cooking. If baking from frozen, add about 20 minutes to cooking time.
13. Let lasagne sit for about 20 minutes before serving (critical for ease of serving).
